meaning: vi.造成麻刺的感觉,兴奋,丁丁作响vt.使感刺痛n.丁当声,麻刺感
examples: The cold air made her face tingle.
I felt a sudden tingle of excitement.
Yes; but you gave him a good box on his ear that made it tingle!
It was later discovered that dual Tingle Creek Chase winner Twist Magic, trained by Paul Nicholls, had fractured a pastern.
As their eyes met, a strange feeling made his entire body tingle.
